svg.text: • Optimal substructure — solution to the whole built from solutions to subproblems
• Overlapping subproblems — same subproblem reached by multiple paths in recursion
If only optimal substructure → divide and conquer (no memoisation needed).
If only overlapping subproblems → memoise but no recurrence simplification.
Both present → DP is the right tool, with exponential-to-polynomial speedup.
Two coding styles: top-down (memoised recursion) or bottom-up (table fill).
1. Define dp[state] — what does dp[i] / dp[i][j] / dp[mask] mean?
2. Recurrence — how does dp[state] decompose into dp[smaller-state]?
3. Base cases — what are dp[0] / dp[empty] / dp[trivial]?
4. Order of evaluation — top-down (recurse + memo) or bottom-up (loop)
5. Final answer — extract from dp[n] / dp[full-state]; reconstruct path if needed
Time = states × work-per-state. Space = states (often reducible to a row or two).
Define the state precisely first; the recurrence usually follows.
